task: I’m helping a friend who’s moving to Stanford for work and wants a realistic shortlist of apartments they could actually consider, so please use Apartments.com to search around Stanford University in Stanford/Palo Alto and keep it to places that look like about a 20-minute commute or less to campus. The budget is pretty specific: for a 1-bedroom, stay under $3,500 a month, and for a 2-bedroom that would work for roommates, stay under $6,000 a month. As you find matches, open the actual listing page for each apartment in its own tab so I can visually compare them later, and make sure each one really shows the rent and bedroom count on the listing itself before you keep it. For commute time, use Google Maps or the map/location details from the listing to estimate how long it would take to get to Stanford University, and only keep the ones that are still roughly within that 20-minute window. I’d like around 20 solid options if possible. Then create a CryptPad Sheets spreadsheet titled Stanford Apartment Options and log each one with the building name or street address, monthly rent, number of bedrooms, estimated commute time to Stanford University, and the direct listing link. Once the sheet is filled out, add a short note in the sheet about which nearby neighborhoods seem to have the most within-budget options, and leave the spreadsheet open along with the apartment tabs so I can look through the listings myself.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully performed a search on Apartments.com for apartments near Stanford University/Palo Alto. The agent's actions (Steps 6-8) involved navigating to the Apartments.com search results for the specified area. The agent then proceeded to collect and verify listings that met the budget criteria (1-bedroom under $3,500 and 2-bedroom under $6,000), as evidenced by the data extraction and the final spreadsheet populated in the CryptPad sheet (visible in the final screenshots). The rubric requirement for a relevant search with aligned criteria is satisfied.

R2: success - The agent successfully opened multiple apartment listing pages in separate browser tabs as required by the rubric. The action history shows the agent iteratively opening and verifying these listings (e.g., steps 9, 12, 15, 18, 36, 41), and the final state confirms that these tabs were maintained alongside the CryptPad spreadsheet.

R3: success - The agent successfully identified and verified multiple apartment listings on Apartments.com. It systematically collected data including building name/address, rent, bedroom count, and commute time, and recorded this information into a CryptPad spreadsheet. The agent verified the rent and bedroom count for the listings by inspecting the page content (as shown in the action history and the final spreadsheet). The spreadsheet contains the required fields and the agent confirmed the listings meet the budget and commute criteria.

R4: success - The agent successfully collected a list of apartments and verified their commute times to Stanford University. The agent used the location/commute data provided on the Apartments.com listing pages (which typically include "Drive: X min" to Stanford University) to filter and verify that each selected apartment was within the 20-minute commute requirement. The final spreadsheet (visible in the second screenshot) explicitly lists an "Estimated commute to Stanford University" column for every entry, with all values being 12 minutes or less, confirming the requirement was met.

R5: success - The agent successfully collected 20 apartment listings, verified their details (rent, bedroom count, and commute time), and logged them into a CryptPad spreadsheet titled "Stanford Apartment Options". The agent also included a note about neighborhoods with the most within-budget options in the spreadsheet. The final state shows the spreadsheet open and the agent confirmed that the listing tabs were maintained.

R6: success - The agent successfully created a CryptPad spreadsheet titled "Stanford Apartment Options". The spreadsheet contains the required columns: building name/address, monthly rent, bedroom count, estimated commute time, and the direct listing link. The agent populated the sheet with the collected apartment data and included a note about the neighborhoods with the most options, as requested. The final screenshot confirms the spreadsheet is populated and correctly titled.

R7: success - The agent successfully created a CryptPad spreadsheet titled "Stanford Apartment Options". The spreadsheet contains the required apartment data and, crucially, includes a note in row 23: "Most within-budget options I found are clustered in Palo Alto (Midtown, Ventura, Evergreen Park, Downtown/Crescent Park) and Menlo Park (Menlo Oak)". The spreadsheet was left open for review.
